commit.message:
8372151: Lilliput: Fix JVM_Clone allocation size for expanded objects and add regression test

JVM_Clone used obj->size() to determine the clone's allocation size, which
includes the extra HeapWord for hash-expanded objects. Since the clone gets a
fresh (not-hashed, not-expanded) mark word, this creates a size mismatch that
crashes Serial GC's linear heap walker.

Fix JVM_Clone to compute the base size using klass->oop_size() with the
source's mark word but with hashctrl bits cleared. This handles both instance
and array clones correctly: for arrays, set_not_hashed_not_expanded() preserves
the array length in the upper 32 bits of the compact mark word.

Add TestStressIHash.java regression test that exercises all 5 GCs (Serial,
Parallel, G1, Shenandoah, ZGC) with compact object headers. The test creates
objects whose layout guarantees hash-expansion, hashes and GC-expands them,
then clones them and verifies clones get independent identity hashes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
